note_strategy.name: slightly_awkward_compliment
note_strategy.description: Reference something specific about the person's LinkedIn profile — a recent post topic, an unusual headline observation, or a genuine niche interest. Must feel written by a human who actually looked at their profile. Avoids the AI-slop pattern of generic praise ('love your work', 'impressed by your journey'). One authentic observation + low-key reason to connect. No pitch in the note — relationship first.
note_type_priority: post_reference — reference a specific post from last 60 days (highest engagement rate — HANDS checks posts tab during search execution)
headline — reference something specific or unusual in their profile headline
interest — reference a genuine niche or hobby listed on their profile
generic — fallback if no posts, headline is bland, and no interests visible
hard_constraints: Under 200 characters (LinkedIn free account limit for connection note)
No mention of email campaign or any prior outreach
No pitch, no service offer in the connection note
No question marks — declarative or statement-based ending ('Good to connect.' not 'Would love to connect?')
No 'I came across your profile' — too generic
IMAP check per lead before sending connection request
Do NOT send if lead replied NOT_INTERESTED or UNSUBSCRIBE in email channel
note_strategy.character_count_note: 200 chars is approx 30-35 words. Keep it to 3 clauses max. Count includes spaces and punctuation.

active_notes_note: Zero active notes. LinkedIn permanently closed for all Batch 5 leads. No active leads with LinkedIn profile data (linkedin_profiles.json never written in 65 campaign sessions). Batch 6 note templates below — activated Day 3 (Fri 2026-06-12, Scenario B confirmed) once HANDS writes batch_6_leads.json (due Mon 2026-06-02 morning — last viable window) and finds LinkedIn profiles.

sector_templates.note: 5 sector-level templates. HANDS selects the most appropriate for each lead, then personalises using actual profile data (post topic, headline text, interest). Confirm under 200 chars before sending. Templates are the starting point — not the final note.

trades_post_reference.template: Saw your post about [specific topic from post]. Refreshing to see a [trade] in [town] talking about [specific element] — most don't. [Name], good to connect.
trades_post_reference.example: Saw your post about underfloor heating jobs in older stone properties. Refreshing to see a Skipton plumber talking about the quirks of Dales builds. David, good to connect.
personalisation_notes: Use the actual post topic — be specific. 'saw your post about boiler installs' is too generic.
Reference the local angle if the post mentions a local job or challenge.
Name at the end is warmer than the start — feels less like a template.

trades_headline.template: Not many [trade]s describe themselves as '[specific unusual element from headline]'. Caught my eye. [Name], good to connect.
trades_headline.example: Not many roofers describe themselves as 'heritage slate specialist'. Caught my eye. James, good to connect.
personalisation_notes: Only use if the headline contains something genuinely specific or unusual.
If the headline is generic ('Plumber | Gas Safe | Harrogate'), use trades_generic instead.
Quote the actual headline text — shows you read it.

trades_generic.template: Based in [town], working with local trades businesses. Thought it worth connecting — [Name], always good to know the decent [trade]s in [area].
trades_generic.example: Based in North Yorkshire, working with local trades businesses. Thought it worth connecting — Mark, always good to know the decent joiners in Knaresborough.
personalisation_notes: Use only as fallback — post_reference and headline are always preferred.
Area can be the town or district — pick whichever sounds more natural.

professional_services_post_reference.template: Your post on [specific topic] was more direct than most [profession] posts tend to be. Appreciated the straightforwardness. [Name], good to connect.
professional_services_post_reference.example: Your post on why small businesses delay getting a will in place was more direct than most solicitor posts tend to be. Appreciated the straightforwardness. Helen, good to connect.
personalisation_notes: Professional services posts are often dry — if they've written something direct or opinion-led, highlight that.
Avoid commenting on 'expertise' or 'knowledge' — sounds sycophantic.
If the post is about a local market topic (Ripon property, Skipton business growth), reference the local angle.

hospitality_bab_generic.template: Running a [hospitality type] in [town] is properly hard work — especially in a destination area. [Name], good to connect with local owners.
hospitality_bab_generic.example: Running a B&B in Grassington is properly hard work — especially in a destination area. Susan, good to connect with local owners.
personalisation_notes: Grassington, Settle, and Skipton all have strong destination/tourism identity — use it.
Acknowledge the difficulty without being patronising.
If they have a post about a specific challenge (Dales flooding, summer bookings, energy bills), use post_reference instead.

leads.warm_nurture_message: Hi [Name], glad we're connected. I work with local tradespeople in North Yorkshire — happy to share a useful resource if relevant sometime. No agenda. [Your name]
Hi [Name], glad we're connected. I work with local businesses in Yorkshire — happy to share something useful if it's relevant. No agenda at all. [Your name]
Hi [Name], glad we're connected. I work with local businesses in the area — happy to share a useful resource if it ever seems relevant. [Your name]
Hi [Name], glad we're connected. I work with North Yorkshire businesses on their online presence — happy to share something useful if relevant. [Your name]
